47.reason: [tmp should be declared as int, not char, because EOF is outside the range of char. This can cause infinite loops or missed EOF on some systems.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  [tmp should be declared as int, not char, because EOF is outside the range of char. This can cause infinite loops or missed EOF on some systems.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  [fgetc returns int, but tmp is char. This can cause incorrect EOF detection, leading to missing last word or infinite loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  [fgetc returns int, but tmp is char; this can cause incorrect EOF detection when char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or missed characters.]

43.reason: [tmp should be declared as int to properly handle EOF, because fgetc returns int. Using char may cause premature loop termination or infinite loop on platforms where char is unsigned.]
